steps.title: Define the emotional evidence
steps.content: The claim is that the banker is very upset. The supporting quotation must show clear emotional distress, not simply an action or setting detail.

steps.title: Reject quotes without distress
steps.content:
• Choice A: Unlocking a door cautiously does not show he is upset.
  • Choice B: The quiet nighttime scene does not reveal the banker's emotions.
  • Choice C: Being delighted is the opposite of being upset.

steps.title: Use tears and sleeplessness
steps.content: A quotation describing the banker lying awake for hours because of his tears and emotion directly illustrates being very upset. The answer is D.
